results.title: GAP analysis & compliance of data centers | TÜV NORD
results.text:
GAP analysis & compliance of data centers | TÜV NORD

# Conformity assessment of data centers

TÜV NORD's conformity assessment of data centers ensures physical security and availability through standards such as TSI, EN 50600, and ISO/IEC 22237. These provide comprehensive requirements definitions for power supply, climate control, building construction, and security systems, enabling companies to demand quality guarantees from colocation providers.

## Ensure physical security and availability through a neutral third party

Particularly in the age of cloud services and co-location offerings, companies are increasingly deciding to outsource their data centers due to the associated costs. This raises the question of quality guarantees. With recognised standards such as TSI, EN 50600 and ISO/IEC 22237 and the corresponding criteria catalogues such as TSI.STANDARD or TSI.EN50600, which were developed by TÜV NORD, tools are available that enable companies to specify requirements, guidelines and expectations of the colocation provider in detail or to request evidence from neutral third parties.

It is not only the power supply and the cooling, air conditioning and ventilation system that play a role when it comes to ensuring the physical security and availability of a data center. TSI offers you a comprehensive and established approach with requirement definitions in the areas of environment, building construction, fire detection and extinguishing systems, security systems and or

results.title: Certification of data centers | TÜV NORD
results.text:
Certification of data centers | TÜV NORD

# Certification of data centers according to TSI, EN 50600, or ISO/IEC 22237

The certification of data centers according to TSI, EN 50600, or ISO/IEC 22237 by TÜV NORD guarantees availability, security, and resilience. Since 2002, over 1,000 projects have been successfully completed, making certified data centers a reliable benchmark in Germany and internationally and creating trust among third-party users.

## Guarantee availability, security, and resilience—with certification for your data center

With data center certification, operators of enterprise data centres and providers of colocation and cloud infrastructures are on the safe side. Their data centers are highly available and stand for reliability thanks to testing by independent third parties. It gives third-party users of such data centers security and confidence in the installation.

TÜV NORD has been carrying out evaluations and certifications of data centres since 2002. Since then, over 1,000 certification projects have been successfully completed. A data center certified by TÜV NORD according to TSI.STANDARD, EN 50600 or ISO/IEC 22237 is a proven benchmark for data centers in Germany and increasingly in the international environment.

Certificates issued to date in accordance with TSI.STANDARD, EN 50600 and ISO/IEC 22237

results.title: EN 50600 – European standard for data centers
results.text:
EN 50600 – European standard for data centers | TÜV NORD

# EN 50600 (DC)

The European standard EN 50600 provides comprehensive guidelines for the planning, construction, and operation of data centers, focusing on power supply, air conditioning, and security infrastructure. TÜV NORD experts support you in designing your data center to be future-proof and state-of-the-art.

## Fundamentals and significance of European standard EN 50600 for data centers

Appropriate measures must be taken to ensure the physical security and availability of IT infrastructures. In this context, the European standard EN 50600 offers a holistic approach to the planning, construction and operation of data centers.

It provides planners, installers and operators of data centers with comprehensive guidelines in the form of various requirements, particularly with regard to power supply, air conditioning and security infrastructure.

Our experts provide comprehensive services to support you in planning, operating or expanding your data center in a future-proof manner based on EN 50600 in accordance with the current state of the art.

EN 50600 Data Center Expert (TÜV®) Training

## EN 50600 overview

EN 50600 is a European series of standards for data centers that uses a holistic approach to provide comprehensive specifications for the planning, construction and operation of data centers.

It defines requirements for the planning of trades such as building construction, power supply, air conditioning, c

results.title: Data Centre PDU Migration & Rack Security | European Bank Case Study
results.text:
Data Centre PDU Migration & Rack Security | European Bank Case Study

# Breaking Vendor Lock-In for a European Bank

### How a major high-street bank transitioned PDU vendors without losing control or visibility

When a leading European bank decided to change its intelligent PDU supplier, the challenge extended far beyond hardware replacement. Furthermore, the bank operated multiple data centre sites and relied on consistent power monitoring and strict rack-level security. Any disruption or fragmentation in management tools was not an option.

Undoubtedly the priority was clear: move to a new PDU vendor while maintaining a single, unified control platform.

REQUEST CASE STUDY

Valid corporate email address

## The Challenge: Change Without Complexity

The bank needed to replace its existing intelligent PDUs after a vendor partnership ended. However, removing all legacy hardware at once was neither practical nor desirable.

Furthermore, running two separate management systems in parallel would increase operational risk, slow response times, and add unnecessary complexity. At the same time, the bank required fully managed rack access control across all sites, with clear governance, auditing, and accountability.

The solution had to support both legacy and new PDUs simultaneously, while keeping power monitoring, environmental data, and security workflows under one roof.

## A Unified Platform for a Phased Transition

Advanced Datacentre Systems (ADS) delivered a vendor-neutral s

results.title: Greenergy and A-Kaabel deploy Vertiv™ rack PDUs to power scalable data center growth in Estonia
results.text:
## Greenergy and A-Kaabel deploy Vertiv™ rack PDUs to power scalable data center growth in Estonia

### Case study

#### Background

Greenergy builds energy-efficient data centers using Estonia’s low carbon energy sources and cold climate to power and cool its facilities. Over time, its vision is to expand operations across the Baltics, harnessing alternative energy sources and proving that high-performance data centers can operate responsibly. Now Estonia’s leading colocation provider, Greenergy targets HPC and AI customers with IT loads exceeding 10 MW, prompting upgrades to its power and liquid cooling infrastructure. Supporting this evolution is A-Kaabel OÜ, an established technology company in Estonia delivering comprehensive design, construction, and maintenance of technical systems, 24/7 infrastructure support and monitoring services across Europe. Since 2020, A-Kaabel has been an authorized sales and service partner for Vertiv in Estonia.

##### Project milestones

• New facility opening: Built and opened a new facility in late 2022. 
• Initial capacity allocation: Allocated 1/3 of the new capacity to customers shortly after opening. 
• Ramp-up of remaining capacity: Ramping up and consuming the remaining capacity based on increasing AI and HPC capacity demands. 
• Future expansion: Well-positioned to promptly react and build out to meet further customer demands. 
Company profile: 
Provider of DC Colocation services 
in Estonia. 
Industry: Data Center 
Region: Estonia

Date：2026-05-29

## Introduction

Choosing a PDU manufacturer affects far more than price or lead time: it shapes uptime, safety, serviceability, and the long-term resilience of your power chain. In high-density racks and critical facilities, small differences in build quality, testing discipline, and compliance standards can determine whether equipment performs reliably under load or becomes a hidden point of failure. results.title: Smart PDU Supplier Selection Guide 2026: Certifications, Real-World Deployments, and Cost Analysis
results.text:
Smart PDU Supplier Selection Guide 2026: Certifications, Real-World Deployments, and Cost Analysis

## How to Select a Smart PDU Supplier for Data Centers: A Technical Buyer's Guide (2026)

Author: HTNXT-Benjamin Hughes-Electrical & Electronics Release time: 2026-05-26 10:55:47 View number: 146

## 1. Introduction

As data center power densities increase and operational efficiency becomes a top priority, the selection of a reliable Smart Power Distribution Unit (Smart PDU) supplier is critical. Procurement professionals must evaluate vendors not only on technical specifications but also on real-world deployment track records, certifications, and support capabilities. This guide provides a structured framework for assessing Smart PDU vendors, with a focus on measurable performance indicators and proven case studies.

## 2. Key Evaluation Criteria for Smart PDU Suppliers

### 2.1 Technical Performance and Measurement Accuracy

A core requirement for any Smart PDU is the accuracy of its electrical parameter measurements. For example, the BOMPDU Intelligent 2nd generation PDU product achieves voltage accuracy of 0.5% within 85–250 V and current accuracy of 0.5% for loads between 3.1 A and 63 A (Level 0.5), as specified in its product datasheet. Similarly, its 1U_1.5U 485 PDU model provides voltage measurement accuracy of Level 1 (1% ± 0.1) and current accuracy of Level 1. These precision levels enable operators to reliably monitor power consumption at the rack level and identify

results.text:
## Search help

**Note:** Searching from the top-level index page will search all documents. Searching from a specific document will search only that document.

* **Find an exact phrase:** Wrap your search phrase in `""` (double quotes) to only get results where the phrase is exactly matched. For example `"PyTorch for the IPU"` or `"replicated tensor sharding"`
* **Prefix query:** Add an `*` (asterisk) at the end of any word to indicate a prefix query. This will return results containing all words with the specific prefix. For example `tensor*`
* **Fuzzy search:** Use `~N` (tilde followed by a number) at the end of any word for a fuzzy search. This will return results that are similar to the search word. N specifies the “edit distance” (fuzziness) of the match. For example `Polibs~1`
* **Words close to each other:** `~N` (tilde followed by a number) after a phrase (in quotes) returns results where the words are close to each other. `N` is the maximum number of positions allowed between matching words. For example `"ipu version"~2`
* **Logical operators.** You can use the following logical operators in a search: 
 * `+` signifies AND operation 
 * `|` signifies OR operation 
 * `-` negates a single word or phrase (returns results _without_ that word or phrase) 
 * `()` controls operator precedence

results.title: OCP Ready™ Data Center Recognition Program » Open Compute Project
results.text:
OCP Ready™ Data Center Recognition Program » Open Compute Project 

Colocation data center operators participating in the OCP Ready™ data center recognition program perform a detailed OCP Ready™ v2 for Hyperscale self assessment of their facilities to ensure they meet the rigorous standards and requirements set forth by hyperscalers. From logistics, site access, security, and base building infrastructure, to network connectivity, the critical aspects of the data center are assessed to ensure alignment with hyperscale companies' facility constraints and are compatible with OCP and industry best practices.

## Scope

There are two self-certifications for OCP Ready™

1. OCP Ready™ v2 for Hyperscale
2. OCP Ready™ v1

Choosing between OCP Ready™ certifications depends on which assessment aligns with your data center specification and target market:

OCP Ready™ v2 for Hyperscale: For data centers catering to hyperscale operators, emphasizing site security, logistics, base building infrastructure, and MWs of power and cooling capacity for large-scale wholesale deployments.

Certification is also available for data centers at varying stages of development:

- Core and Shell - Forecasted - (shell complete by date)
- Core and Shell Ready (ready for fit-out by date)
- Multi-tenant Build in Progress - (estimated live services by date)
- Multi-tenant Capacity (available by date)

There is a self-assessment required for each certification

1. OCP Ready™ v1: For colocation data centers meet

results.text:
Global Data Center Supplier Risk Management | Achilles

Case Studies

# Building a Scalable, Sustainable, and Resilient Data Center Construction Network

Google’s Data Center Construction Program team uses Achilles to scale its global data center construction program to deliver consistent supplier risk management. The platform streamlines supplier intake, prequalification, and risk visibility, enabling faster, data-driven procurement decisions while supporting sustainability, DEI, and multi-region growth.

“The Achilles platform strengthens our Data Center Construction Program by delivering a more comprehensive and structured prequalification process, even as the volume of contractors entering the program continues to grow. By incorporating broader supplier data, expanded risk metrics, and centralized platform capabilities—including reporting and insights—we have enhanced compliance visibility and consistency in our contractor evaluations while maintaining strong governance and controls.”

> Erick Hudtwalcker, Program Manager, Strategic Risk Management, Google Data Centers Business Controls

## Scaling Data Center Construction Through Consistent Supplier Risk Management

As Google continues to expand its global data center footprint to support growing demand for cloud services and artificial intelligence, the Data Center Construction Program organization is evolving how it engages, qualifies, and manages its data center construction suppliers and wider supplier ecosystem. results.title: GDC Ready platform partners  |  Google Distributed Cloud  |  Google Cloud Documentation
results.text:
GDC Ready platform partners | Google Distributed Cloud | Google Cloud Documentation

# GDC Ready platform partners Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.

This page is for Admins and architects who define IT solutions and system architecture in accordance with company strategy in coordination with key stakeholders. To learn more about common roles and example tasks that we reference in Google Cloud content, see Common GKE user roles and tasks.

GDC Ready platform qualification

GDC Ready platform validation ensures that our partners' hardware platforms support the latest version and provide the foundation for Google Distributed Cloud (software only) on bare metal and VMware. This validation includes testing the following:

- The functions most reliant on the underlying hardware platform such as the ability to deploy and scale a cluster
- The successful interaction of GDC with the platform partner's compute, network, and storage solution
- The ability to deploy on-premises and expose a workload through a supported load balancer option on GDC clusters on the partner platform

GDC Ready platform partners have also demonstrated a strong understanding of GDC architectures, its features, and how customers benefit from all components of GDC. Google commits to working with GDC Ready platform partners to ensure that they have the most up-to-date validation regarding GDC and its roadmap, and their hardware works with the latest version of

results.title: About the Solution Providers - OCP Ready™ Program
results.text:
About OCP Ready » Open Compute Project

OCP Ready™ v2 for Hyperscale and OCP Ready™ v1 Data Center Recognition Program - A collaborative initiative forged by hyperscalers specifically for colocation providers. Developed by our Data Center Facility (DCF) Project Team and drawing upon the collective wisdom and experience of the hyperscale community, this program provides clear guidelines and best practices tailored to the unique requirements of colocation environments.

Colocation providers participating in the OCP Ready™ program undergo a detailed self assessment and DCF Project Team approval process, where their facilities are evaluated to ensure they meet the rigorous standards set forth by hyperscalers. From logistics, site access and base building infrastructure, to network connectivity, every aspect of the data center is scrutinized to ensure alignment with hyperscale companies' facility constraints and are compatible with OCP and industry best practices. This allows colocation providers the ability to market their facilities directly to the hyperscale audience.

There are two categories for OCP Ready™

1. OCP Ready™ v1
2. OCP Ready™ v2 for Hyperscale

Upon successful completion of the assessment process, colocation providers receive the designation as an OCP OCP Ready™ for Hyperscale v2 or OCP Ready™ v1 facility, a testament to their commitment to innovation, efficiency, and sustainability. OCP Ready™ facilities are listed on the OCP Marketplace and promoted through vari
